source_citation: OurWorldinData.org/low-carbon-electricity-by-country | CC BY. Data source: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025).
chart_title: Share of electricity generated by low-carbon sources
subtitle_verbatim: Measured as a percentage of total electricity produced in the country or region. Low-carbon sources correspond to renewables and nuclear power, that produce significantly less greenhouse-gas emissions than fossil fuels. Renewables include solar, wind, hydropower, bioenergy, geothermal, wave, and tidal.
notes: Our World in Data line chart, 8 country/region series with line+marker style. Legend labels appear at the right end of each line (not in a box) with L-shaped gray connectors to color-matched text. Y-axis labels formatted as 'NN%' at 0/20/40/60/80/100. X-axis labels: 1985, 1990, 1995, 2000, 2005, 2010, 2015, 2020, 2025 (endpoint labels 1985 and 2025 are nudged inward by ~15 px from the actual data extent). The y-axis is continuous linear (0-100%) with evenly spaced ticks at 77 px per 20 percentage points; the visual whitespace between 60% and 80% is empty data space, NOT a broken/discontinuous axis (verified by uniform gridline pixel spacing). Sweden and France sit in the 80-100% band; United Kingdom, Germany, United States, World, China, and India sit in the 0-65% band. Dark blue United States line is occluded by United Kingdom (1993-1994) and by World (2012-2014); those 5 points are linearly interpolated. China 1986 occluded by overlapping cluster, also interpolated. Germany series begins at 1986.
